chemistry.reaction_name: Flow photochemistry review - multiple reactions covered

substrates.substrate_A: Various substrates depending on reaction type

photocatalyst.name: Various including Rose Bengal, Ru(bpy)3Cl2, TiO2, Eosin Y

chemistry.product: Various products depending on reaction type
chemistry.scope_summary: Comprehensive review covering microflow and macroflow photochemistry including [2+2] cycloadditions, photooxygenations, photocatalytic reactions, rearrangements, and visible-light photocatalysis. Multiple reactor designs and light sources discussed.

figures.key_information: Photochemical reactor setup with internal light source producing blue/cyan illumination, glass reaction vessel with multiple inlet/outlet connections
figures.caption: Figure 1:  An immersion-well batch reactor with 125 W medium pres- sure Hg lamp.
Figure 2:  Transmission profile of a 0.05 M solution, ε = 200 M −1  cm −1 .
Figure 2:  Transmission profile of a 0.05 M solution, ε = 200 M −1  cm −1 .

figures.flow_path: serpentine/meandering channel pattern with multiple parallel segments
figures.key_information: Blue light source positioned above a serpentine microfluidic chip with three inlet connections and three outlet connections
figures.caption: Figure 3:  Schematic of a typical microflow photochemical reactor (above) and detail of a triple-channel microflow reactor (below) used for the photooxygenation of citronellol [20].

figures.key_information: Continuous flow photochemical reactor setup with external UV/blue light illumination of coiled reactor tube
figures.caption: Figure 4:  Schematic of a typical macroflow photochemical reactor (above) and images of the FEP photochemical flow reactor developed by Booker- Milburn and Berry [21].

figures.flow_path: oxygen and solution flows merge in T-mixer creating segmented flow with alternating oxygen and solution segments
figures.key_information: segmented flow reactor with oxygen gas and solution creating alternating segments for enhanced mass transfer
figures.caption: Scheme 37:  Schematic of the Seeberger photooxygenation apparatus and sensitised photooxygenation of citronellol.

figures.flow_path: Two separate flows (oxygen and solution) are mixed in T-mixer then flow through coiled reactor to collection vessel
figures.key_information: Gas-liquid segmented flow system with oxygen and solution segments alternating through coiled reactor
figures.caption: Scheme 37:  Schematic of the Seeberger photooxygenation apparatus and sensitised photooxygenation of citronellol.

translation_logic.batch_limitation: Poor light penetration, over-irradiation, scaling difficulties
translation_logic.flow_advantage: Overcomes light penetration issues, enables better control and efficient synthetic photochemistry on preparative scales
translation_logic.what_changed: Reactor geometry from batch vessels to continuous flow reactors including microfluidic chips and coiled tubes
translation_logic.what_stayed_same: Photochemical reaction mechanisms and light sources
translation_logic.safety_improvement: Better control over reaction conditions and heat management

figure_descriptions: Immersion-well batch reactor with 125 W mercury lamp
Microflow photochemical reactor with serpentine chip
Macroflow photochemical reactor with coiled tube

notes: This is a comprehensive review paper covering multiple photochemical reactions and reactor designs rather than a single specific reaction optimization. Limited quantitative data available as expected for a review article.
